Reduces the stereo width of the incoming signal
notes: At zero the signal is reduced to mono at a pan position set by the 'Initial pan' parameter

parameter_description: Sets the pan position of the mono-ized signal
notes: 0 is fully left. 127 is fully right

parameter_description: Attenuates the input signal fed to the tape write head
Sets the amount of 'inertia' or slew on the pitch CV input
notes: At zero the tape speed follows the pitch input closely. At the maximum value pitch changes are quite gradual

parameter_description: When set to '1' the tape speed is set to zero
notes: Note that the Pitch inertia affects how quickly the tape stops and starts

parameter_description: When set to '1' the tape is reversed
notes: Note that the Pitch inertia affects how quickly the tape reverses
When set to 1 only input 1 is used as a mono input (leaving input 2 free for parameter mapping)
usage: 0: Inputs 1 and 2 used as stereo input; 1: Input 1 is used as a mono input

parameter_description: Controls the sample rate at which the algorithm runs
notes: At 0 the algorithm runs at 48kHz which doubles the maximum delay time. At 1 the algorithm runs at full speed (96kHz)

parameter_description: Controls the bit depth used in the delay memory
notes: It is not the bit depth used in any other processing. Settings this to '0' (16 bit) doubles the maximum delay time

parameter_description: The fade time to use when in Inertia free mode
The CV input to use for pitch or 0 for none
The CV input to use as the clock or 0 for none
notes: The delay time is set as the time between two rising clock edges

parameter_description: When switched from 0 to 1 the algorithm acts on a tap tempo event
notes: The 'Tap tempo' parameter allows for a tap tempo function. Typically you would map this to a button push or a MIDI event. By default it is mapped to the 'L' button

parameter_description: Sets the number of consistent clocks required to change the delay time
notes: By raising the value the manual '1' you're telling the algorithm to only change the delay time when it receives a number of clocks of the same time in succession ('same' here means within 10%) - so it will ignore the rogue clock you get from stopping and starting the transport

A second order state variable filter is available within the delay feedback path. The 'Filter type' parameter lets you smoothly fade between the various responses – 'thru' (i.e. No filtering) low pass band pass high pass and back to thru

parameter_description: Enables the effects loop and sets in position the signal flow
notes: Enabling the 'FX Loop position' (i.e. Setting it to something other than Off) breaks the internal delay feedback path and sends it out of and back into the module allowing you to insert other effects or processing into the delay

parameter_description: Instigates a rapid clear of the delay buffer (while maintaining passthrough of the dry signal)
Enables the saturation processing (on the tape output before the filter)
Sets the depth of the saturation effect by applying gain before the saturation waveshaper
Controls the shape of the saturation
notes: At '127' the effect is that of soft saturation and clipping. At '0' the effect is of hard digital clipping
usage: 0~127: Hard digital clipping to soft saturation and clipping
